meaning: the Roman god of fire and metalworking
sentences: Vulcan was the Roman god of fire and metalworking. Vulcan 是羅馬神話中的火與鍛造之神。
The museum placed a statue of Vulcan near the furnace hall. 博物館把 Vulcan 的雕像放在鍛爐大廳附近。

meaning: a Roman festival held in honor of Vulcan
sentences: Vulcanalia was a Roman festival held in honor of Vulcan. Vulcanalia 是為了敬奉 Vulcan 而舉行的羅馬節慶。

meaning: relating to Vulcanalia

meaning: relating to Vulcan or Vulcanalia

meaning: relating to Vulcan or volcanoes

meaning: volcanic; of Vulcan
sentences: The Vulcanic rock still bore the marks of heat. 那塊 Vulcanic 岩石仍帶著熱力的痕跡。
They used Vulcanic as a poetic word for fiery. 他們把 Vulcanic 當作帶有詩意的火熱形容詞。

meaning: the state or quality of volcanic activity
sentences: The island's vulcanicity fascinated the research team. 那座島的火山活動性讓研究團隊著迷。
A map of vulcanicity showed the hottest zones. 火山活動性的地圖標出了最活躍的區域。

meaning: volcanic activity
sentences: The textbook described vulcanism as the activity of volcanoes. 教科書把 vulcanism 描述為火山的活動。
Students studied vulcanism alongside plate tectonics. 學生把 vulcanism 與板塊構造一起學習。

meaning: a person who studies volcanoes or advocates volcanic theory
sentences: The vulcanist watched the crater for signs of eruption. 那位火山學者觀察火山口是否有噴發跡象。
An early vulcanist argued that heat shaped the rocks. 一位早期的火山學者主張熱力塑造了岩石。

meaning: hard black rubber, or a volcanic rock
sentences: The old comb was made of vulcanite. 那把舊梳子是用硬黑橡膠做的。

sentences: The chemist selected a vulcanizable rubber compound. 化學家選了一種可硫化的橡膠配方。

meaning: the product of vulcanization
sentences: The lab stored the vulcanizate in a sealed tray. 實驗室把 vulcanizate 放在密封托盤裡。
A strong vulcanizate can resist heat and wear. 堅固的 vulcanizate 可以抵抗高溫與磨損。

meaning: the process of treating rubber to make it tougher and more durable
sentences: Vulcanization made the rubber tougher and less sticky. 硫化讓橡膠更堅韌，也沒那麼黏。
The factory used vulcanization to improve the tires. 工廠用硫化來提升輪胎品質。

meaning: to treat rubber with heat and sulfur to make it tougher
sentences: They vulcanize the rubber to make it last longer. 他們會把橡膠硫化，讓它更耐用。
The workshop will vulcanize the strip before shipping. 工作坊會在出貨前把那條橡膠帶硫化。

meaning: a person or machine that vulcanizes
sentences: The vulcanizer worked beside the heater. 那台硫化機在加熱器旁運作。

meaning: relating to volcanology
sentences: The vulcanological report focused on the crater rim. 那份火山學報告聚焦在火山口邊緣。
She kept a vulcanological notebook during the trip. 她在旅行中一直寫著火山學筆記。

meaning: a scientist who studies volcanoes
sentences: The vulcanologist visited the island after the eruption. 那位火山學家在噴發後前往那座島。
A vulcanologist can read the layers of ash. 火山學家可以讀懂火山灰層。

meaning: the study of volcanoes
sentences: Vulcanology combines chemistry, geology, and field work. 火山學結合了化學、地質學與實地考察。
The student wrote a paper on vulcanology. 那名學生寫了一篇關於火山學的報告。

meaning: 庸俗的，粗俗的
sentences: He was criticized for his vulgar jokes and inappropriate behavior. 他因其庸俗的笑話和不恰當的行為而受到批評。

meaning: 普通分數
sentences: A vulgar fraction consists of a numerator and a denominator. 普通分數由分子和分母組成。
Students learn how to simplify a complex vulgar fraction in math class. 學生在數學課上學習如何化簡複雜的普通分數。

meaning: common; ordinary; vulgar
sentences: The Latin adjective vulgare means common or ordinary. 拉丁文形容詞 vulgare 的意思是 common 或 ordinary。
The writer used vulgare to describe everyday speech. 那位作家用 vulgare 來描述日常語言。

meaning: a vulgar or uncultured person
sentences: The novel portrayed him as a vulgarian. 那本小說把他描寫成一個粗俗的人。
A vulgarian may speak loudly and rudely in public. 粗俗的人在公共場合可能說話大聲又無禮。

meaning: somewhat vulgar
sentences: The joke sounded slightly vulgarish to the audience. 那個笑話對觀眾來說稍微有點粗俗。
She avoided a vulgarish tone in the final draft. 她在定稿裡避免了粗俗的語氣。

meaning: a vulgar word, phrase, or expression
sentences: The editor removed the vulgarism from the paragraph. 編輯把那個粗俗用語從段落中刪掉。
Old dictionaries sometimes list a vulgarism as slang. 舊字典有時會把粗俗用語列為俚語。

meaning: a person who uses vulgar language or habits
sentences: The critic called him a vulgarist. 評論家稱他是個粗俗的人。
A vulgarist often offends careful readers. 粗俗的人常常冒犯講究的讀者。

meaning: the quality of being vulgar
sentences: The speaker's vulgarity surprised the room. 演講者的粗俗讓整個房間都很驚訝。
She tried to avoid vulgarity in the script. 她努力避免劇本中出現粗俗。

meaning: the act of making something common or simplified
sentences: The textbook criticized the vulgarization of the idea. 教科書批評了這個觀念被過度簡化。
Some scholars worry about vulgarization in popular articles. 有些學者擔心流行文章中的過度簡化。

meaning: to make something common or simpler
sentences: Writers sometimes vulgarize complex ideas for general readers. 作家有時會把複雜概念簡化給一般讀者看。
The teacher refused to vulgarize the original argument. 老師拒絕把原本的論點過度簡化。

meaning: one who vulgarizes
sentences: The critic saw him as a vulgarizer of serious art. 評論家認為他是嚴肅藝術的簡化者。
A vulgarizer can make a hard topic sound careless. 把內容簡化的人可能讓難題聽起來很隨便。

meaning: resembling something vulgar
sentences: The vulgarlike comment made the meeting awkward. 那句近乎粗俗的評論讓會議變得尷尬。
She crossed out the vulgarlike phrase. 她把那個近似粗俗的短語劃掉。

meaning: in a vulgar way
sentences: He spoke vulgarly during the argument. 他在爭論中說話很粗俗。
The student wrote the reply vulgarly and regretfully. 那名學生用粗俗而懊悔的語氣寫下回覆。

meaning: the state of being vulgar
sentences: The novel hints at the character's vulgarness. 那本小說暗示了角色的粗俗。
They disliked the vulgarness of the old slogan. 他們不喜歡那句舊標語的粗俗感。

meaning: in a vulgar or common manner
sentences: He explained the issue vulgarwise for the class. 他用很通俗的方式向全班解釋這個問題。
The note was written vulgarwise rather than formally. 那份筆記是以通俗方式寫成，而不是正式文體。

meaning: the Latin translation of the Bible
sentences: The Vulgate is the Latin translation of the Bible. Vulgate 是聖經的拉丁文譯本。
The professor compared the Vulgate with Greek manuscripts. 教授把 Vulgate 和希臘文手稿做比較。

meaning: the common or standard version of a text
sentences: The vulgate text became the standard version in that tradition. 那個俗本成了該傳統中的標準版本。
Editors treated the vulgate as the common reading. 編輯把這個俗本當作常見的讀法。

meaning: the common people
sentences: The essay used vulgus to mean the common people. 那篇文章用 vulgus 指平民大眾。
In old Latin, vulgus carried a broad social meaning. 在古拉丁文中，vulgus 帶有廣泛的社會意義。

meaning: a wound; an injury
sentences: The note used vuln as a shorthand for wound. 那份筆記把 vuln 當作 wound 的簡寫。
A clinician wrote vuln in the margin. 一位臨床人員把 vuln 寫在頁邊。

meaning: the state of being vulnerable
sentences: The plan reduced the system's vulnerability. 這個方案降低了系統的脆弱性。
She worried about the vulnerability of the old bridge. 她擔心那座老橋的脆弱程度。

meaning: 脆弱的、易受傷的
sentences: Small businesses are often the most vulnerable during an economic crisis. 在經濟危機期間，小企業往往是最脆弱的。
We must take steps to protect the most vulnerable members of our community. 我們必須採取措施保護社區中最脆弱（易受傷害）的成員。

meaning: the quality of being vulnerable
sentences: The poet wrote about human vulnerableness. 那位詩人寫下人類的脆弱性。
Its vulnerableness made the creature easy to protect. 它的脆弱性讓這個生物很容易被保護。

meaning: in a vulnerable way
sentences: He spoke vulnerably about his fear. 他以脆弱的語氣談到自己的恐懼。
The child looked vulnerably at the door. 那個孩子脆弱地望著門口。

meaning: wound-healing; healing wounds
sentences: The herb was prized for its vulnerary effect. 這種草藥因其傷口癒合效果而珍貴。
The doctor described the ointment as vulnerary. 醫生把那種藥膏形容為有癒傷作用。

meaning: to wound or injure
sentences: The sharp metal can vulnerate the skin. 鋒利的金屬會傷到皮膚。
They did not want to vulnerate the patient during the procedure. 他們不想在程序中傷到病人。

meaning: the act of wounding
sentences: The report mentioned a small vulneration on the arm. 報告提到手臂上有一處小傷口。
Doctors cleaned the vulneration carefully. 醫師小心清理那處傷口。

meaning: causing or tending to wound
sentences: The filter had a vulnerative edge. 那個過濾器的邊緣帶有傷害性。
The poet used vulnerative to describe the harmful force. 那位詩人用 vulnerative 來描述那股有害的力量。

meaning: full of wounds; wounded or sore
sentences: The old painting showed a vulnerose figure after many battles. 那幅舊畫裡的人物在經歷多次戰鬥後看起來滿身傷痕。
His vulnerose hands told the story of years of hard labor. 他那雙傷痕累累的手訴說了多年辛苦工作的故事。

meaning: wounding; causing injury
sentences: The surgeon avoided a vulnific cut near the nerve. 外科醫生避免在神經附近做會造成傷害的切口。
The poet used vulnific to describe the cruel remark. 詩人用 vulnific 來形容那句殘酷的話。

meaning: wounding; harmful
sentences: The writer called the rumor vulnose because it spread harm. 作者把那則謠言稱作 vulnose，因為它帶來傷害。
A vulnose comment can hurt more than a physical blow. 一句帶傷害性的話有時比拳頭更傷人。

meaning: a small fox; also the name of a constellation
sentences: Vulpecula is a faint constellation in the northern sky. Vulpecula 是北方天空中一個較暗的星座。
The guide compared the shape of Vulpecula to a tiny fox. 導覽書把 Vulpecula 的形狀比作一隻小狐狸。

meaning: fox-like; resembling a fox
sentences: The creature had a vulpecular face and a sharp stare. 那個生物有著狐狸般的臉和銳利的眼神。
Her vulpecular smile made the story feel sly and clever. 她那像狐狸一樣的微笑讓故事帶上狡黠的氣息。

meaning: a member of the fox family or a fox-like animal
sentences: The old taxonomy listed the animal as a Vulpeculid. 舊分類法把那種動物列為 Vulpeculid。
A Vulpeculid was described as nimble and alert. Vulpeculid 被描述成敏捷而警覺的生物。

meaning: a genus of foxes
sentences: The red fox belongs to the genus Vulpes. 赤狐屬於 Vulpes 這個屬。
Scientists compared different species within Vulpes. 科學家比較了 Vulpes 屬內的不同物種。

meaning: fox-like; resembling a fox
sentences: He had a vulpic look that made people trust him less. 他帶著狐狸般的神情，讓人更難信任他。
The author gave the villain a vulpic grin. 作者替反派安排了一個像狐狸一樣的笑容。

meaning: killing foxes; intended to kill foxes
sentences: The trap was criticized as a vulpicidal device. 那個陷阱被批評為一種會殺狐的裝置。
They banned vulpicidal poisons in the reserve. 他們在保護區內禁止使用會毒殺狐狸的毒物。

meaning: the killing of foxes; a fox killer
sentences: The report condemned vulpicide in the hunting district. 那份報告譴責了獵區中獵殺狐狸的行為。
He was described as a vulpicide in the old folklore tale. 在那個古老民間故事裡，他被描寫成一名殺狐者。

meaning: the practice or act of killing foxes
sentences: The article discussed vulpicidism as a hunting practice. 那篇文章把獵殺狐狸的做法當成一種狩獵行為來討論。
Modern conservation groups oppose vulpicidism. 現代保育團體反對獵殺狐狸。

meaning: a fox subfamily
sentences: Vulpinae is used in older zoological classification. Vulpinae 是較早期動物分類中的一個名稱。
The textbook grouped foxes under Vulpinae. 那本教科書把狐狸歸在 Vulpinae 之下。

meaning: fox-like; crafty or sly
sentences: He gave a vulpine smile and changed the subject. 他露出狐狸般的微笑，然後轉移了話題。
The novel describes the spy as vulpine and patient. 那本小說把間諜描寫成狡猾而耐心的人。

meaning: fox-like cunning or sly behavior
sentences: The essay criticized his vulpinism in politics. 那篇文章批評了他在政治上的狐狸式狡黠。
Her vulpinism made every negotiation difficult. 她那種狡猾的作風讓每次談判都很難進行。

meaning: a mineral variety of anhydrite
sentences: The geologist labeled the sample as vulpinite. 地質學家把那塊樣本標為 vulpinite。
Vulpinite can appear in pale, granular masses. Vulpinite 有時會呈現淡色、顆粒狀的團塊。

meaning: a forceps-like surgical instrument used for gripping tissue
sentences: The doctor used a vulsella to hold the tissue in place. 醫生用 vulsella 夾住組織固定位置。
A vulsella is designed for a firm surgical grip. vulsella 是為了在手術中牢固夾持而設計的。

meaning: a small forceps-like surgical instrument
sentences: The tray held a vulsellum beside the scissors. 托盤上除了剪刀，還放著一把 vulsellum。
A vulsellum can be used for delicate tissue handling. vulsellum 可用於精細地夾持組織。

meaning: a mineral species or mineral variety
sentences: The specimen was cataloged as vulsinite in the archive. 那件標本在檔案中被編為 vulsinite。
Collectors rarely encounter vulsinite in the field. 收藏家在野外很少遇到 vulsinite。

meaning: a vulture
sentences: The old text used Vultur as a Latinized name for a vulture. 舊文獻把 Vultur 當作兀鷲的拉丁化名稱。
A Vultur circled high above the dry plain. 一隻 Vultur 在乾燥平原上空盤旋。

meaning: 從他人的 苦難 protlem="proti";
sentences: Within ten minutes of the accident, the vultures appeared and were organizing lawsuits. 事故發生後不到十分鐘， 禿鷹 就出現了，並正在組織訴訟。 事故發生後不到十分鐘，禿鷹就出現了，並且正在組織訴訟。 事故發生後不到十分鐘，禿鷹就出現了，正在進行組織訴訟。
The term vulture refers to A person who profits from the suffering of others. 禿鷹一詞是指 從他人的 苦難>>中 獲利 的人。 禿鷹一詞指的是從他人的痛苦中獲利的人。 禿鷹活動是指從他人的交易>>中獲利的人。

meaning: resembling a vulture
sentences: The reporter described the crowd's vulturelike behavior. 記者把那群人的行為形容得像兀鷲一樣。
His vulturelike patience made the deal uncomfortable. 他那種像兀鷲般的耐心讓交易變得不舒服。

meaning: in a vulture-like manner
sentences: They watched the market vulturewise for any weakness. 他們像兀鷲一樣盯著市場，等待任何弱點。
The rivals moved vulturewise toward the failing company. 競爭對手像兀鷲般朝那家瀕臨失敗的公司逼近。

meaning: the vulture family
sentences: The birds were classified under Vulturidae in the old text. 那些鳥在舊書裡被歸到 Vulturidae。
Researchers compared feeding habits across Vulturidae species. 研究人員比較了 Vulturidae 各物種的覓食習性。

meaning: the vulture subfamily
sentences: The museum label placed the specimen in Vulturinae. 博物館標籤把那件標本歸入 Vulturinae。
Vulturinae includes several large scavenging birds. Vulturinae 包含幾種大型食腐鳥。

meaning: vulture-like; resembling a vulture
sentences: The statue had a vulturine beak and a stern face. 那座雕像有著兀鷲般的喙和嚴峻的臉。
She gave him a vulturine glare across the room. 她隔著房間投來兀鷲般冷峻的目光。

meaning: having the habits or appearance of a vulture
sentences: The critic called the investors vulturish and opportunistic. 評論者把那些投資者形容成像兀鷲一樣機會主義。
A vulturish silhouette hovered over the dead tree. 一個兀鷲般的黑影盤旋在枯樹上方。

meaning: vulture-like behavior; scavenging greed
sentences: The article warned against vulturism in disaster zones. 文章警告不要在災區表現出兀鷲式的趁火打劫。
His vulturism made the whole negotiation feel predatory. 他那種像兀鷲般的作風讓整場談判充滿掠奪感。

meaning: a southeast wind
sentences: The sailors feared the sudden vulturn over the sea. 水手們害怕海面上突然吹起的東南風。
A dry vulturn swept across the coast in the afternoon. 下午，一陣乾燥的東南風吹過海岸。

meaning: vulture-like; scavenging or predatory
sentences: The landlord's vulturous tactics angered the community. 房東像兀鷲般的手段激怒了社區。
The writer used vulturous to describe the reporters' curiosity. 作者用 vulturous 來形容記者們像兀鷲一樣的窺探。

meaning: the external female genitalia
sentences: The doctor explained the anatomy of the vulva in class. 醫師在課堂上講解了外陰的解剖結構。
Regular hygiene helps protect the vulva from irritation. 適當清潔有助於保護外陰免於刺激。

meaning: relating to the vulva
sentences: The nurse noted a vulval rash in the chart. 護理師在病歷上記錄了外陰皮疹。
Vulval discomfort can have several different causes. 外陰不適可能有好幾種不同原因。

meaning: relating to the vulva
sentences: The clinic treated a mild vulvar infection. 診所治療了一個輕微的外陰感染。
She reported vulvar pain after the exam. 她在檢查後回報外陰疼痛。

meaning: shaped like a vulva; opening wide
sentences: The flower had a vulvate opening that narrowed quickly. 那朵花有一個外形像外陰的開口，之後迅速收窄。
The old botanical note described the shell as vulvate. 舊植物筆記把那個殼描述成 vulvate 的形狀。

meaning: shaped like the vulva
sentences: The rare structure was described as vulviform in the report. 那個罕見構造在報告中被描述成 vulviform。
A vulviform opening appeared at the center of the specimen. 樣本中央出現一個 vulviform 的開口。

meaning: inflammation of the vulva
sentences: The doctor treated her vulvitis with careful instructions. 醫師用詳細的指示治療她的外陰炎。
Vulvitis can cause redness, swelling, and pain. 外陰炎可能造成紅腫與疼痛。

meaning: relating to the vulva and the thigh or groin
sentences: The old medical text mentioned vulvocrural irritation. 舊醫學文獻提到外陰與大腿根部的刺激。
A vulvocrural fold was noted during the examination. 檢查時注意到一條外陰與股溝相關的皺摺。

meaning: relating to the vulva and the uterus
sentences: The report described a rare vulvouterine condition. 那份報告描述了一種罕見的外陰與子宮相關病況。
Vulvouterine anatomy is discussed in advanced medical texts. 高階醫學書中會討論外陰與子宮的關係。

meaning: relating to the vulva and vagina
sentences: The patient had a vulvovaginal infection that needed treatment. 病人有外陰陰道感染，需要治療。
The doctor explained vulvovaginal hygiene carefully. 醫師仔細說明了外陰陰道的清潔方式。

meaning: inflammation of the vulva and vagina
sentences: The child was diagnosed with vulvovaginitis after the exam. 那名孩子在檢查後被診斷為外陰陰道炎。
Vulvovaginitis can cause itching, pain, and discharge. 外陰陰道炎可能引起搔癢、疼痛和分泌物。

meaning: a rare or obsolete term or sound-form in older dictionaries
sentences: The old glossary listed vum as an obscure entry. 舊詞彙表把 vum 列為一個冷僻條目。
Few readers now know what vum means without a dictionary. 現在幾乎沒人不查字典就知道 vum 的意思。

meaning: competing eagerly; trying to win
sentences: Several teams are vying for the championship this year. 幾支隊伍今年正競逐冠軍。
The two companies were vying for the same contract. 兩家公司正在爭取同一份合約。

meaning: in a competing or eager-to-win manner
sentences: The students vyingly raised their hands for the prize. 學生們爭先恐後地舉手想要拿獎品。
The candidates spoke vyingly to win support. 候選人們用競爭的口吻發言，希望爭取支持。
